slices.name: LintFinding
slices.code:
116 | class LintFinding:
 117 |     __slots__ = ("level", "rule_id", "message", "fragment", "autofix")
 118 | 
 119 |     def __init__(
 120 |         self,
 121 |         level: str,
 122 |         rule_id: str,
 123 |         message: str,
 124 |         fragment: str = "",
 125 |         autofix: Optional[str] = None,
 126 |     ) -> None:
 127 |         self.level = level          # "error" | "warning"
 128 |         self.rule_id = rule_id
 129 |         self.message = message
 130 |         self.fragment = fragment    # the offending portion of the command
 131 |         self.autofix = autofix      # rewrite suggestion, or None
 132 | 
 133 |     def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 134 |         d: Dict[str, Any] = {
 135 |             "rule_id": self.rule_id,
 136 |             "message": self.message,
 137 |         }
 138 |         if self.fragment:
 139 |             d["fragment"] = self.fragment
 140 |         if self.autofix:
 141 |             d["autofix"] = self.autofix
 142 |         return d

slices.name: __init__
slices.code:
153 |     def __init__(
 154 |         self,
 155 |         tool: str,
 156 |         script_token: str,
 157 |         raw_line: str,
 158 |         flags_present: frozenset,

slices.name: _normalise_command_text
slices.code:
174 | def _normalise_command_text(text: str) -> List[str]:
 175 |     """Collapse PS1/bash line continuations and return logical command lines."""
 176 |     logical: List[str] = []
 177 |     pending: List[str] = []
 178 | 
 179 |     for raw_line in text.splitlines():
 180 |         stripped = raw_line.strip()
 181 |         # Discard pure comment lines
 182 |         if stripped.startswith("#"):
 183 |             continue
 184 |         # Discard empty lines (unless we're accumulating)
 185 |         if not stripped and not pending:
 186 |             continue
 187 |         # PS1 backtick continuation
 188 |         if stripped.endswith("`"):
 189 |             pending.append(stripped[:-1].rstrip())
 190 |             continue
 191 |         # Bash backslash continuation
 192 |         if stripped.endswith("\\") and not stripped.endswith("\\\\"):
 193 |             pending.append(stripped[:-1].rstrip())
 194 |             continue
 195 |         # End of a logical line
 196 |         pending.append(stripped)
 197 |         joined = " ".join(pending).strip()
 198 |         if joined:
 199 |             logical.append(joined)
 200 |         pending = []
 201 | 
 202 |     # Anything left in pending
 203 |     if pending:
 204 |         joined = " ".join(pending).strip()
 205 |         if joined:
 206 |             logical.append(joined)
 207 | 
 208 |     return logical

slices.name: _tokenise
slices.code:
211 | def _tokenise(line: str) -> List[str]:
 212 |     """Tokenise a single logical command line using shlex (posix=False)."""
 213 |     try:
 214 |         raw = shlex.split(line, posix=False)
 215 |     except ValueError:
 216 |         # Unmatched quote — fall back to simple split
 217 |         raw = line.split()
 218 |     # Strip outer quotes from each token
 219 |     result: List[str] = []
 220 |     for tok in raw:
 221 |         if len(tok) >= 2 and tok[0] == tok[-1] and tok[0] in ('"', "'"):
 222 |             tok = tok[1:-1]
 223 |         result.append(tok)
 224 |     return result

slices.name: _identify_tool
slices.code:
227 | def _identify_tool(script_token: str) -> str:
 228 |     stem = Path(script_token).stem.lower()
 229 |     if "create_file_map_v2" in stem:
 230 |         return TOOL_V2
 231 |     if "create_file_map_v3" in stem:
 232 |         return TOOL_V3
 233 |     if "semantic_slicer" in stem:
 234 |         return TOOL_SLICER
 235 |     if "manifest_doctor" in stem:
 236 |         return TOOL_DOCTOR
 237 |     if "tool_command_linter" in stem:
 238 |         return TOOL_LINTER
 239 |     return TOOL_UNKNOWN

slices.name: _is_interpreter
slices.code:
242 | def _is_interpreter(tok: str) -> bool:
 243 |     stem = Path(tok).stem.lower()
 244 |     return stem in ("python", "python3", "py", "python3.11", "python3.10", "python3.12")

slices.name: _parse_args_for_tool
slices.code:
251 | def _parse_args_for_tool(
 252 |     tokens: List[str],
 253 |     tool: str,
 254 | ) -> Tuple[frozenset, Dict[str, Any], List[str]]:
 255 |     """
 256 |     Walk the token list and classify tokens as:
 257 |       - flag (starts with -)
 258 |       - flag value (follows a value-taking or nargs flag)
 259 |       - positional
 260 | 
 261 |     Returns (flags_present, flag_values, positionals).
 262 |     """
 263 |     if tool == TOOL_SLICER:
 264 |         value_flags = _SLICER_VALUE_FLAGS
 265 |         bool_flags = _SLICER_BOOL_FLAGS
 266 |         nargs_flags = _SLICER_NARGS_FLAGS
 267 |     elif tool == TOOL_V2:
 268 |         value_flags = _V2_VALUE_FLAGS
 269 |         bool_flags = _V2_BOOL_FLAGS
 270 |         nargs_flags = _V2_NARGS_FLAGS
 271 |     elif tool == TOOL_V3:
 272 |         value_flags = _V3_VALUE_FLAGS
 273 |         bool_flags = _V3_BOOL_FLAGS
 274 |         nargs_flags = _V3_NARGS_FLAGS
 275 |     else:
 276 |         value_flags = frozenset()
 277 |         bool_flags = frozenset()
 278 |         nargs_flags = frozenset()
 279 | 
 280 |     flags_present: set = set()
 281 |     flag_values: Dict[str, Any] = {}
 282 |     positionals: List[str] = []
 283 | 
 284 |     i = 0
 285 |     while i < len(tokens):
 286 |         tok = tokens[i]
 287 | 
 288 |         # --flag=value form
 289 |         if tok.startswith("-") and "=" in tok:
 290 |             flag, _, val = tok.partition("=")
 291 |             flags_present.add(flag)
 292 |             flag_values[flag] = val
 293 |             i += 1
 294 |             continue
 295 | 
 296 |         # Boolean (store_true) flag
 297 |         if tok in bool_flags:
 298 |             flags_present.add(tok)
 299 |             i += 1
 300 |             continue
 301 | 
 302 |         # nargs="*" / nargs="+" flags: consume non-flag tokens
 303 |         if tok in nargs_flags:
 304 |             flags_present.add(tok)
 305 |             vals: List[str] = []
 306 |             i += 1
 307 |             while i < len(tokens) and not tokens[i].startswith("-"):
 308 |                 vals.append(tokens[i])
 309 |                 i += 1
 310 |             flag_values[tok] = vals
 311 |             continue
 312 | 
 313 |         # Value-taking flag: consume next token as value
 314 |         if tok in value_flags:
 315 |             flags_present.add(tok)
 316 |             if i + 1 < len(tokens) and not tokens[i + 1].startswith("-"):
 317 |                 flag_values[tok] = tokens[i + 1]
 318 |                 i += 2
 319 |             else:
 320 |                 i += 1
 321 |             continue
 322 | 
 323 |         # Unknown flag — consume next token if it looks like a value
 324 |         if tok.startswith("-"):
 325 |             flags_present.add(tok)
 326 |             if i + 1 < len(tokens) and not tokens[i + 1].startswith("-"):
 327 |                 flag_values[tok] = tokens[i + 1]
 328 |                 i += 2
 329 |             else:
 330 |                 i += 1
 331 |             continue
 332 | 
 333 |         # Positional token
 334 |         positionals.append(tok)
 335 |         i += 1
 336 | 
 337 |     return frozenset(flags_present), flag_values, positionals

slices.name: extract_invocations
slices.code:
344 | def extract_invocations(logical_lines: List[str]) -> List[InvocationRecord]:
 345 |     """
 346 |     Find all recognised tool invocations across the logical command lines.
 347 |     An invocation begins with a Python interpreter token followed by a script token.
 348 |     """
 349 |     invocations: List[InvocationRecord] = []
 350 | 
 351 |     for line in logical_lines:
 352 |         tokens = _tokenise(line)
 353 |         if not tokens:
 354 |             continue
 355 | 
 356 |         # Find python interpreter token (may be first or after 'py' wrapper)
 357 |         idx = 0
 358 |         if _is_interpreter(tokens[0]):
 359 |             idx = 1
 360 |         else:
 361 |             continue  # line does not start with a Python interpreter
 362 | 
 363 |         if idx >= len(tokens):
 364 |             continue
 365 | 
 366 |         script_token = tokens[idx]
 367 |         tool = _identify_tool(script_token)
 368 |         if tool == TOOL_UNKNOWN:
 369 |             continue
 370 | 
 371 |         arg_tokens = tokens[idx + 1:]
 372 |         flags_present, flag_values, positionals = _parse_args_for_tool(arg_tokens, tool)
 373 | 
 374 |         invocations.append(InvocationRecord(
 375 |             tool=tool,
 376 |             script_token=script_token,
 377 |             raw_line=line,
 378 |             flags_present=flags_present,
 379 |             flag_values=flag_values,
 380 |             positionals=positionals,
 381 |         ))
 382 | 
 383 |     return invocations

slices.name: _get_output_value
slices.code:
390 | def _get_output_value(inv: InvocationRecord) -> Optional[str]:
 391 |     """Return the output path value from any known output flag, or None."""
 392 |     for flag in (*_FILEMAP_OUTPUT_FLAGS, *_SLICER_OUTPUT_FLAGS):
 393 |         if flag in inv.flag_values:
 394 |             return str(inv.flag_values[flag])
 395 |     return None

slices.name: _output_is_reingestion
slices.code:
398 | def _output_is_reingestion(path: str) -> bool:
 399 |     return bool(_REINGESTION_RE.search(path))

slices.name: rule_r001
slices.code:
402 | def rule_r001(inv: InvocationRecord) -> Optional[LintFinding]:
 403 |     """R001: create_file_map_v2.py does not support -o; using it is always an error."""
 404 |     if inv.tool == TOOL_V2 and "-o" in inv.flags_present:
 405 |         return LintFinding(
 406 |             level="error",
 407 |             rule_id=R001,
 408 |             message=(
 409 |                 "create_file_map_v2.py does not accept -o. "
 410 |                 "The flag will be treated as an unrecognised argument and the command will fail. "
 411 |                 "Use --out instead."
 412 |             ),
 413 |             fragment=inv.raw_line,
 414 |             autofix="Replace -o <value> with --out <value>",
 415 |         )
 416 |     return None

slices.name: rule_r002
slices.code:
419 | def rule_r002(inv: InvocationRecord) -> Optional[LintFinding]:
 420 |     """R002: create_file_map_v3.py -o is deprecated; prefer --out."""
 421 |     if inv.tool == TOOL_V3 and "-o" in inv.flags_present and "--out" not in inv.flags_present:
 422 |         return LintFinding(
 423 |             level="warning",
 424 |             rule_id=R002,
 425 |             message=(
 426 |                 "create_file_map_v3.py accepts -o as a deprecated alias for --out. "
 427 |                 "The command will run but will emit a deprecation warning. "
 428 |                 "Prefer --out for clarity and forward compatibility."
 429 |             ),
 430 |             fragment=inv.raw_line,
 431 |             autofix="Replace -o <value> with --out <value>",
 432 |         )
 433 |     return None

slices.name: rule_r003
slices.code:
436 | def rule_r003(inv: InvocationRecord) -> Optional[LintFinding]:
 437 |     """R003: slicer positional '.' without --manifest — broad scan risk (WARN).
 438 | 
 439 |     WARN rather than BLOCK because a broad '.' scan is technically valid when
 440 |     the user explicitly intends to scan the whole tree. The risk is that it
 441 |     produces enormous file sets (as seen in the transcript: 110k-file scans).
 442 |     The linter warns rather than blocks to avoid rejecting legitimate wide scans.
 443 |     """
 444 |     if (
 445 |         inv.tool == TOOL_SLICER
 446 |         and "." in inv.positionals
 447 |         and "--manifest" not in inv.flags_present
 448 |         and "--git-diff" not in inv.flags_present
 449 |     ):
 450 |         return LintFinding(
 451 |             level="warning",
 452 |             rule_id=R003,
 453 |             message=(
 454 |                 "Semantic slicer is invoked with a broad positional '.' and no --manifest. "
 455 |                 "This may scan the entire working tree and produce very large bundles. "
 456 |                 "Consider using --manifest to restrict the file set."
 457 |             ),
 458 |             fragment=inv.raw_line,
 459 |             autofix="Add --manifest <path_to_csv> and remove the positional '.'",
 460 |         )
 461 |     return None

slices.name: rule_r004
slices.code:
464 | def rule_r004(inv: InvocationRecord) -> Optional[LintFinding]:
 465 |     """R004: slicer --manifest + positional '.' — contradictory and produces a massive scan."""
 466 |     if (
 467 |         inv.tool == TOOL_SLICER
 468 |         and "." in inv.positionals
 469 |         and "--manifest" in inv.flags_present
 470 |     ):
 471 |         return LintFinding(
 472 |             level="error",
 473 |             rule_id=R004,
 474 |             message=(
 475 |                 "Semantic slicer has both --manifest and a positional '.' argument. "
 476 |                 "The slicer will merge the manifest file list with the broad '.' scan, "
 477 |                 "producing a superset that defeats the purpose of the manifest. "
 478 |                 "Remove the positional '.' when --manifest is specified."
 479 |             ),
 480 |             fragment=inv.raw_line,
 481 |             autofix="Remove the positional '.' from the argument list",
 482 |         )
 483 |     return None

slices.name: rule_r005
slices.code:
486 | def rule_r005(inv: InvocationRecord) -> Optional[LintFinding]:
 487 |     """R005: automated slicer invocation missing --deterministic (WARN).
 488 | 
 489 |     WARN rather than BLOCK because --deterministic is a best-practice flag for
 490 |     reproducibility, not a hard correctness requirement. The command will run
 491 |     correctly without it; the output will simply carry a variable timestamp.
 492 |     """
 493 |     if inv.tool != TOOL_SLICER:
 494 |         return None
 495 |     is_automated = bool(inv.flags_present & _SLICER_AUTOMATION_FLAGS)
 496 |     if not is_automated:
 497 |         # Also flag if the output name looks like an automated bundle artefact
 498 |         out_val = _get_output_value(inv)
 499 |         if out_val and _output_is_reingestion(out_val):
 500 |             is_automated = True
 501 |     if is_automated and "--deterministic" not in inv.flags_present:
 502 |         triggers = sorted(inv.flags_present & (_SLICER_AUTOMATION_FLAGS | {"-o", "--output"}))
 503 |         return LintFinding(
 504 |             level="warning",
 505 |             rule_id=R005,
 506 |             message=(
 507 |                 "Automated or scripted slicer invocation detected "
 508 |                 f"({', '.join(triggers) if triggers else 'output name pattern'}). "
 509 |                 "--deterministic is not set; the bundle will include a variable "
 510 |                 "timestamp, making repeated runs non-reproducible."
 511 |             ),
 512 |             fragment=inv.raw_line,
 513 |             autofix="Add --deterministic to the slicer command",
 514 |         )
 515 |     return None

slices.name: rule_r006
slices.code:
518 | def rule_r006(inv: InvocationRecord) -> Optional[LintFinding]:
 519 |     """R006: output path matches a re-ingestion pattern (WARN).
 520 | 
 521 |     WARN rather than BLOCK because names like '_bundle_' could be intentional.
 522 |     The rule catches accidental re-ingestion of generated artefacts, not all
 523 |     uses of bundle-like naming conventions.
 524 |     """
 525 |     out_val = _get_output_value(inv)
 526 |     if out_val and _output_is_reingestion(out_val):
 527 |         return LintFinding(
 528 |             level="warning",
 529 |             rule_id=R006,
 530 |             message=(
 531 |                 f"Output path '{out_val}' matches a pattern associated with "
 532 |                 "generated bundle artefacts (e.g. _bundle_, _Extraction, timestamps). "
 533 |                 "Writing tool output to a bundle-named path risks re-ingestion "
 534 |                 "in a future scan."
 535 |             ),
 536 |             fragment=out_val,
 537 |             autofix=None,
 538 |         )
 539 |     return None

slices.name: rule_r007_for_file
slices.code:
542 | def rule_r007_for_file(invocations: List[InvocationRecord]) -> Optional[LintFinding]:
 543 |     """R007: command file has a broad slicer invocation without a manifest_doctor step.
 544 | 
 545 |     Only fires when analysing a command file (multiple invocations); a single
 546 |     inline command would not include a manifest_doctor step by design.
 547 |     WARN rather than BLOCK because the manifest_doctor may have been run
 548 |     separately in an earlier session.
 549 |     """
 550 |     has_doctor = any(inv.tool == TOOL_DOCTOR for inv in invocations)
 551 |     has_broad_slicer = any(
 552 |         inv.tool == TOOL_SLICER and "." in inv.positionals
 553 |         for inv in invocations
 554 |     )
 555 |     if has_broad_slicer and not has_doctor:
 556 |         return LintFinding(
 557 |             level="warning",
 558 |             rule_id=R007,
 559 |             message=(
 560 |                 "Command file contains a broad slicer invocation but no "
 561 |                 "manifest_doctor.py step. Running manifest_doctor before the slicer "
 562 |                 "detects pollution, verifies required coverage, and prevents "
 563 |                 "large unfiltered scans."
 564 |             ),
 565 |             fragment="(command file level)",
 566 |             autofix="Add a manifest_doctor.py invocation before the slicer command",
 567 |         )
 568 |     return None

slices.name: _apply_rules
slices.code:
575 | def _apply_rules(
 576 |     invocations: List[InvocationRecord],
 577 |     is_file: bool,
 578 |     disabled_rules: frozenset,
 579 | ) -> List[LintFinding]:
 580 |     findings: List[LintFinding] = []
 581 | 
 582 |     per_invocation_rules = [rule_r001, rule_r002, rule_r003, rule_r004, rule_r005, rule_r006]
 583 | 
 584 |     for inv in invocations:
 585 |         for rule_fn in per_invocation_rules:
 586 |             finding = rule_fn(inv)
 587 |             if finding and finding.rule_id not in disabled_rules:
 588 |                 findings.append(finding)
 589 | 
 590 |     # File-level rule
 591 |     if is_file and R007 not in disabled_rules:
 592 |         finding = rule_r007_for_file(invocations)
 593 |         if finding:
 594 |             findings.append(finding)
 595 | 
 596 |     return findings

slices.name: _determine_status
slices.code:
599 | def _determine_status(findings: List[LintFinding]) -> str:
 600 |     if any(f.level == "error" for f in findings):
 601 |         return "BLOCK"
 602 |     if any(f.level == "warning" for f in findings):
 603 |         return "WARN"
 604 |     return "PASS"

slices.name: build_report
slices.code:
607 | def build_report(
 608 |     findings: List[LintFinding],
 609 |     command_source: str,
 610 |     status: str,
 611 |     invocation_count: int,
 612 | ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 613 |     errors = [f.to_dict() for f in findings if f.level == "error"]
 614 |     warnings = [f.to_dict() for f in findings if f.level == "warning"]
 615 |     autofixes = [
 616 |         f.autofix for f in findings if f.autofix
 617 |     ]
 618 |     return {
 619 |         "status": status,
 620 |         "safe_to_run": status != "BLOCK",
 621 |         "command_source": command_source,
 622 |         "invocations_analysed": invocation_count,
 623 |         "errors": errors,
 624 |         "warnings": warnings,
 625 |         "autofix_suggestions": autofixes,
 626 |     }

slices.name: build_rewrite_text
slices.code:
629 | def build_rewrite_text(
 630 |     findings: List[LintFinding],
 631 |     command_source: str,
 632 | ) -> str:
 633 |     lines: List[str] = [
 634 |         "# Tool Command Linter — Rewrite Suggestions",
 635 |         f"# Source: {command_source}",
 636 |         "",
 637 |     ]
 638 |     actionable = [f for f in findings if f.autofix]
 639 |     if not actionable:
 640 |         lines.append("# No autofix suggestions available.")
 641 |         return "\n".join(lines)
 642 | 
 643 |     for finding in actionable:
 644 |         lines.append(f"# [{finding.rule_id}] {finding.message}")
 645 |         if finding.fragment and finding.fragment != "(command file level)":
 646 |             lines.append(f"# Fragment: {finding.fragment}")
 647 |         lines.append(f"# Suggestion: {finding.autofix}")
 648 |         lines.append("")
 649 | 
 650 |     lines.append("# NOTE: This file lists suggestions only. No original command was modified.")
 651 |     return "\n".join(lines)

slices.name: parse_args
slices.code:
658 | def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
 659 |     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
 660 |         description=(
 661 |             "Lint Aletheia tool commands for unsafe or invalid patterns. "
 662 |             "Never executes the command being linted."
 663 |         )
 664 |     )
 665 |     source_group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
 666 |     source_group.add_argument(
 667 |         "--command", metavar="CMD",
 668 |         help="Inline command string to lint.",
 669 |     )
 670 |     source_group.add_argument(
 671 |         "--command-file", metavar="PATH",
 672 |         help="Path to a command file (.ps1, .sh, or plain text) to lint.",
 673 |     )
 674 |     parser.add_argument(
 675 |         "--config", metavar="PATH",
 676 |         help="Optional JSON config file (may disable rules or add custom patterns).",
 677 |     )
 678 |     parser.add_argument(
 679 |         "--out", default="command_lint_report.json",
 680 |         help="JSON report output path (default: command_lint_report.json).",
 681 |     )
 682 |     parser.add_argument(
 683 |         "--rewrite-out", metavar="PATH", dest="rewrite_out",
 684 |         help="Optional path to write human-readable rewrite suggestions. "
 685 |              "Original command/file is never modified.",
 686 |     )
 687 |     return parser.parse_args()

slices.name: main
slices.code:
690 | def main() -> int:
 691 |     args = parse_args()
 692 | 
 693 |     # --- Load optional config ---
 694 |     disabled_rules: frozenset = frozenset()
 695 |     extra_reingestion: List[str] = []
 696 | 
 697 |     if args.config:
 698 |         try:
 699 |             cfg = load_json_config(Path(args.config))
 700 |         except (FileNotFoundError, ConfigError) as exc:
 701 |             print(f"Error loading config: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
 702 |             return 1
 703 |         disabled_raw = cfg.get("disabled_rules", [])
 704 |         disabled_rules = frozenset(str(r) for r in disabled_raw)
 705 |         extra_reingestion = cfg.get("extra_reingestion_patterns", [])
 706 |         if extra_reingestion:
 707 |             global _REINGESTION_RE
 708 |             combined = _REINGESTION_PATTERNS + extra_reingestion
 709 |             _REINGESTION_RE = re.compile("|".join(combined), re.IGNORECASE)
 710 | 
 711 |     # --- Load command text ---
 712 |     if args.command:
 713 |         command_text = args.command
 714 |         command_source = "inline"
 715 |         is_file = False
 716 |     else:
 717 |         cmd_path = Path(args.command_file)
 718 |         if not cmd_path.exists():
 719 |             print(f"Error: command file not found: {cmd_path}", file=sys.stderr)
 720 |             return 1
 721 |         command_text = cmd_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
 722 |         command_source = cmd_path.name
 723 |         is_file = True
 724 | 
 725 |     # --- Parse ---
 726 |     logical_lines = _normalise_command_text(command_text)
 727 |     invocations = extract_invocations(logical_lines)
 728 | 
 729 |     # --- Apply rules ---
 730 |     findings = _apply_rules(invocations, is_file=is_file, disabled_rules=disabled_rules)
 731 |     status = _determine_status(findings)
 732 |     report = build_report(findings, command_source, status, len(invocations))
 733 | 
 734 |     # --- Write JSON report ---
 735 |     write_json_report(report, Path(args.out))
 736 | 
 737 |     # --- Write rewrite suggestions (optional, never modifies original) ---
 738 |     if args.rewrite_out:
 739 |         rewrite_text = build_rewrite_text(findings, command_source)
 740 |         Path(args.rewrite_out).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 741 |         Path(args.rewrite_out).write_text(rewrite_text, encoding="utf-8")
 742 |         print(f"Rewrite suggestions written to: {args.rewrite_out}")
 743 | 
 744 |     print(f"Lint status: {status} "
 745 |           f"({len([f for f in findings if f.level == 'error'])} errors, "
 746 |           f"{len([f for f in findings if f.level == 'warning'])} warnings)")
 747 | 
 748 |     return 2 if status == "BLOCK" else 0
